# Knowledge Base Shelf-Index

Generated by `/sdlc-knowledge-base:kb-rebuild-indexes`. This file is the librarian agent's first-read on every query — it identifies which library files are relevant before deep-reading them.

**Do not edit this file by hand.** Run `/sdlc-knowledge-base:kb-rebuild-indexes` after editing library files to update.

This shelf-index ships as the starter pack for `sdlc-knowledge-base`. The three library files it references are example content drawn from the Agentic SDLC research programme. Replace or extend with library files relevant to your own problem space.

---

## 1. agent-suitability-rubric.md

**Hash:** placeholder-regenerated-on-first-run-via-kb-rebuild-indexes
**Terms:** rubric, dora, evaluation, scoring, agent-suitability, methodology-comparison, BDD, TDD, specification-driven, continuous-delivery, trunk-based-development, scrum, kanban, waterfall, PRINCE2, mob-programming, VeriAct, AgentsWay, Spec-Kit-SDD, Phase 1 rubric, 14 dimensions, agent rework ratio, specification-ambiguity incidents
**Facts:**
- 14-dimension weighted rubric (140 total points), 4 highest-weight dimensions account for 49 points / 35% — Spec Formality, Task Decomposability, V&V Rigour, Feedback Loop Structure (Phase 1, 2026)
- BDD scores 67/100 across all three contexts and is the highest-scoring single methodology with high evidence confidence (Phase 5, 2026)
- Top compositions cluster at 68-71/140 — even the "best" agentic SDLC is mediocre by the rubric (Phase 5 synthesis)
- Confidence varies dramatically: traditional methodologies have 40+ years of empirical evidence; AI-native frameworks have 1-2 years (Phase 5 evidence quality)
- Specification formality is the strongest single predictor of agent suitability across all delivery contexts
**Links:** library/agentic-sdlc-options.md, library/specification-formality-and-agent-performance.md, EPIC-#97-commissioning

## 2. agentic-sdlc-options.md

**Hash:** placeholder-regenerated-on-first-run-via-kb-rebuild-indexes
**Terms:** sdlc, agentic, scrum, safe, formal-methods, solo-development, multi-team, assured, SpecFlow Solo, Agile-Native SDLC, SAFe-Lite Agentic, Assured Agentic, decision matrix, blast radius, regulatory burden, specification maturity, migration paths
**Facts:**
- Four distinct SDLC options span the scale/assurance/regulatory spectrum: SpecFlow Solo (1-2), Agile-Native (3-10), SAFe-Lite (multi-team), Assured (regulated) — Agentic SDLC research 2026
- Decision rule: start at Option 2 (Agile-Native) as default; jump to Option 4 (Assured) if any one of {physical harm risk, regulator audit, catastrophic failure cost} is true — quick selection heuristic
- All four options compose proven building blocks (TDD/BDD/TBD/ADRs/Scrum/SAFe/Cleanroom) with emerging agent practices (RACI, prompt registry, escalation protocols, 1-2 years field evidence)
- Migration is mostly one-directional: Option 1→2 common as projects mature; 2→3 when single product becomes multi-team platform; 2/3→4 invasive, triggered by regulation
- Common foundations are non-negotiable across all four options: machine-readable specs, automated verification, trunk-based, ADRs, agent RACI, prompt/model registry
**Links:** library/agent-suitability-rubric.md, library/specification-formality-and-agent-performance.md, EPIC-#97-commissioning

## 3. specification-formality-and-agent-performance.md

**Hash:** placeholder-regenerated-on-first-run-via-kb-rebuild-indexes
**Terms:** specifications, BDD, gherkin, openapi, formal-methods, TLA+, AWS, DynamoDB, Cosmos DB, Cloudflare, agent rework, specification-driven, narrative-stories, executable-contracts, Z notation, B method, VDM, AsyncAPI, protobuf, Coq, Isabelle
**Facts:**
- Specification formality is the highest-weighted dimension in the agent suitability rubric (14/140 points, Phase 1 2026)
- TLA+ caught 6 critical bugs in DynamoDB before launch; specification effort ~4 weeks per system; defect cost averted ~6-12 months debugging (AWS 2018, Communications of the ACM)
- BDD agent suitability score 67/100 — highest-scoring single methodology with high evidence confidence (Phase 5)
- Five-level formality scale from informal narrative (1) to highly formal executable (5); each step measurably reduces agent escalation rates
- Agent rework ratio target <30%; above this, specs are too informal (Phase 5 quality signals)
**Links:** library/agent-suitability-rubric.md, library/agentic-sdlc-options.md, EPIC-#97-commissioning, EPIC-#105-knowledge-base
